data.title: Opendoor stock rallies as Fed suggests rate cuts could boost housing market. Powell hints at potential cuts for struggling economy.
data.description: The stock of Opendoor Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: OPEN) experienced a significant surge following Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's dovish comments at Jackson Hole. Powell's statement highlighted rising risks to the U.S. job market and hinted at potential interest rate cuts if the economy continues to slow. This resulted in a positive response from investors, with OPEN shares rising as part of the broader market rally. Advocates for Opendoor, like Anthony Pompliano, expressed optimism about the impact of interest rate cuts on the company, noting the potential for growth in the housing market. More details on this story can be found at Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trump fired the BLS commissioner following a weaker-than-expected jobs report, intensifying skepticism around government economic data.
data.description: The former president, Donald Trump, acted on his longstanding doubts about government economic data by dismissing the commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics following a jobs report that fell short of expectations.
Market prices are surging, signaling growing concerns of another bubble. Stay vigilant. #FinancialNews

data.title: Trump wants Fed to cut borrowing costs to lower mortgage rates; Jay Powell resists.Characters: 76
data.description: The President is urging the Federal Reserve to reduce interest rates in order to lower borrowing costs for individuals. He believes that doing so will specifically benefit potential homebuyers by bringing down mortgage rates.

data.title: Trump's economic approval ratings similar to former President Biden's.
data.description: The economic approval ratings of President Donald Trump are now comparable to those of his predecessor.

data.title: Weaknesses in labor market threaten Trump and Fed as private sector warning signs suggest labor force may struggle with higher rates.
data.description: Indicators from the private sector suggest potential challenges ahead, casting doubt on the ability of the labor force to withstand prolonged higher interest rates.

data.title: Trump pressures Fed Chair Powell, administration probes Federal Reserve's renovation costs at headquarters.
data.description: The pressure from President Donald Trump on Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell will escalate further on Thursday as administration officials plan to visit the central bank headquarters. This visit is part of an investigation into the increasing cost of the renovation project, with the price tag for the project steadily rising.

data.title: Policymakers aim to increase publicly traded companies for investor access to potential giants like Alphabet, Meta, Tesla.
data.description: For a considerable span of time, government officials have endeavored to increase the quantity of companies listed on the stock exchange, providing ordinary investors with an opportunity to invest in potential future giants like Alphabet, Meta, or Tesla.

data.description: Lawmakers in the upcoming 2026 midterm elections are now prioritizing an obscure financial regulatory problem that has quickly grown in importance. This issue has become a key consideration for politicians dealing with tight races, as they aim to address the matter swiftly to appeal to voters.

data.title: Trump urges Fed Chair to consider cost of growing debt service payments during upcoming meeting.
data.description: President Donald Trump is urging Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell to take into account the impact of the government’s increasing debt service payments during the upcoming central bank meeting. Trump's concern revolves around the growing burden on the government as debt payments continue to rise. The meeting is scheduled to take place later this month as the government faces a significant debt service cost, which has reached [insert specific amount] in recent years. Trump's request highlights the importance of managing the debt obligations effectively to ensure financial stability and sustainability.

data.title: DHL suspends U.S. shipments due to customs chaos after Trump removed exemption on tariffs for goods under $800.
data.description: One of the leading global courier services has decided to suspend its operations following President Trump's removal of an exemption on tariffs for items worth less than $800 being imported. This decision comes as a response to the new tariff regulations set by the President.

data.title: Fed governor Lisa Cook facing mortgage fraud allegations; Trump admin calls for resignation.
data.description: Officials from the Trump administration have accused the Federal Reserve governor of engaging in mortgage fraud. They have urged for her resignation following these allegations.

data.title: Investment bank Cantor Fitzgerald initiates coverage on ACG Metals with 'Buy (Speculative)' rating.
data.description: ACG Metals Ltd. revealed on August 22, 2025, that Cantor Fitzgerald Canada Corporation has begun equity research coverage on the Company. This move signifies a significant development for ACG as Cantor is a renowned global investment bank. The initiation of equity research coverage by Cantor will provide crucial insights and analysis on ACG's performance and growth prospects moving forward. This strategic partnership highlights the Company's growing presence in the market and indicates a positive outlook for investors and stakeholders.

data.title: Trump extends TikTok ban to mid-September unless ownership changes. This is the fourth delay in taking action.
data.description: The popular short-video platform has been granted until mid-September to modify its ownership model or risk being prohibited in the United States. President Trump has given several extensions to the initial deadline.

data.title: Workday stock down 3.64% to $219.30 despite strong Q2 results. Analysts lower price targets but maintain positive ratings.
data.description: Workday Inc's shares on NASDAQ dropped by 3.64% to $219.30 despite a strong fiscal second-quarter report. Analysts responded with varying sentiments, with BofA Securities maintaining a Buy rating but reducing the price target from $278 to $265. KeyBanc Capital Markets slashed the price target from $325 to $285 but retained an Overweight rating. Canaccord Genuity reduced the price target from $330 to $275 while retaining a Buy rating. RBC Capital Markets reiterated an Outperform rating with a price target of $340. Guggenheim Securities analyst John DiFucci maintained a Neutral rating. Workday’s Q2 results showed a 400 basis points operating margin expansion to 29%, and strong revenue growth fueled by existing base and net new ACV, with AI-driven ACV doubling year-on-year. Sills noted the strength of Workday's platform. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
